zh.Tudeley-cum-Capel, Tonbridge, Kent, Engl: 英國英格蘭肯特圖德勒
zh.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth De Ros Roos於1404年出生在英國艾可拉德文特。她於1413年與John of Brenchley Burr在英國英格蘭艾可拉德文特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1452年在英國肯特布藍利享年48歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth De Ros Roos于1404年出生在英国艾可拉德文特。她于1413年与John of Brenchley Burr在英国英格兰艾可拉德文特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1452年在英国肯特布藍利享年48岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ros
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.
Swedish: ornamental name from ros ‘rose’.

variants.name: Rus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Simion, Grigore, Doina, Dorel, Ilie, Liviu, Petru, Vasile.

Czech, Slovak, and Romanian (of Slavic origin): ethnic name for a Russian, ultimately derived from an Old Norse term meaning ‘oarsman, rower’ (from the root roths-), a reference to the fact that the Russian state was established by Scandinavians who rowed their ships up rivers inland from the Baltic sea. In some cases this may also be origin of the Slovenian and Croatian surname (see 3 below).
Czech: descriptive nickname from the adjective rusý ‘blond’.

variants.name: Ross
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Roos
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch (also De Roos): topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a rose, or a metonymic occupational name for someone who grew roses, from roos ‘rose’.
Dutch: from the female personal name Rosa (from Latin rosa ‘rose’).
Dutch: nickname from roos ‘erysipelas’, an infection which causes reddening of the skin and scalp, applied presumably to someone with a ruddy complexion.

variants.name: Roose
meanings: Dutch: variant of   Roos   1–2.
German: variant of   Rose   and   Roos  .
English: variant of   Ross   1.

variants.name: Roosen
meanings: Dutch and Flemish: patronymic from   Roos   1–3.

variants.name: Rose
meanings: English, Scottish, French, Walloon, Danish, and German: from the name of the flower, Middle English, Old French, Middle High German rose (from Latin rosa), in various applications. In part, it is a topographic name for someone who lived at a place where wild roses grew, or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house bearing the sign of the rose. It is also found, especially in Europe, as a nickname for a man with a ‘rosy’ complexion (compare 4 below). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and similar-sounding names from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Rózsa (see Rozsa ), Slovak Róža and Czech Roza . Compare 6 below and French Larose 2.
English: from the Middle English female personal name Rohese, Roese, later Rose, Royse (ancient Germanic Hrodohaidis, Rothaid, composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + haid(is) ‘kind, sort’).
English and Scottish: variant of   Ross  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth De Ros Roos was born in 1404, in Radwinter, Essex, England. She married John of Brenchley Burr in 1413, in Radwinter, Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1452, in Brenchley, Kent, England, at the age of 48.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth De Ros Roos est née en 1404, à Radwinter, Essex, Angleterre. Elle a épousé John of Brenchley Burr en 1413, à Radwinter, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1452, à Brenchley, Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 48 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth De Ros Roos wurde 1404 in Radwinter, Essex, England geboren. Sie hat 1413 in Radwinter, Essex, England, United Kingdom John of Brenchley Burr geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1452 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Brenchley, Kent, England.

it.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth De Ros Roos è nata il nel 1404 nel Radwinter, Essex, England. Lei sposato John of Brenchley Burr nel 1413, nel Radwinter, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1452, nel Brenchley, Kent, England, all'età di 48 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth De Ros Roosは1404年にイギリスエセックスラドウィンターで生まれた。1413年にイギリスイギリスエセックスラドウィンターでJohn of Brenchley Burrと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1452年にイギリスケントブレンチリーで48歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth De Ros Roos(은)는 1404년 영국 에세스 라드윈터에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1413년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 라드윈터에서 John of Brenchley Burr(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1452년 영국 켄트 브렌클리에서 48세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth De Ros Roos родилась в 1404 г. в местности радвинтир, эссекс, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John of Brenchley Burr в 1413 г. в местности радвинтир, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1452 г. в местности бренчли, кэнт, Англия в возрасте 48 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth De Ros Roos nació en 1404, en Radwinter, Essex, Inglaterra. Se casó con John of Brenchley Burr en 1413, en Radwinter, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1452, en Brenchley, Kent, Inglaterra, a la edad de 48 años.
